You just completed a Slay the Spire 2 run as the silent.
Result: DEFEAT at Floor 33 (fitness: 106.3)
Combats won: 14/14
Run duration: 5747.4s

## Full-run Decision Digest

## Strategic Thread (agent intent at decision time — hypotheses, not ground truth)
### F1
- [event] Foundation phase: Look for an early shop or event to remove the curse from Neow's Bones, while leveraging our two new Neow relics to aggressively hunt elites and build a solid damage core for Act 1.
- [card_select] Foundation plan: survive with frontload damage and efficient block while looking for a scaling engine. Prioritize cheap draw or high-impact damage.
- [card_select] Foundation plan: survive with frontload damage and efficient block while looking for a scaling engine. Use Blade of Ink for early damage, look for strong frontload attacks and draw to complement it.
- [map] Foundation phase: focus on drafting efficient damage cards like Dagger Throw or Poisoned Stab and high-value block to survive Act 1 Elites; prioritize card rewards over early shops.
### F2
- [card_reward] Foundation plan: Survive Act 1 by adding efficient frontload damage like Ricochet and Pinpoint. Look for discard outlets for Ricochet or a core scaling engine (Shivs/Poison). Avoid adding more 2-cost or 3-cost cards unless they win fights instantly.
### F4
- [event] Foundation phase: use recent gold to remove strikes/curses or buy immediate power at a shop to stabilize from our early HP loss.
### F5
- [card_reward] Foundation plan: Survive Act 1 by adding efficient frontload damage like Ricochet and discard outlets like Dagger Throw to play it for free, while leaning into our early Shiv generation.
- [map] Foundation phase: focusing on drafting efficient damage and block to stabilize health; prioritizing monster fights for card rewards over early shops to build a cohesive engine.
### F6
- [hand_select] Neutralize can eliminate one enemy for free after this discard resolves.
- [card_reward] Foundation plan: Survive Act 1 with efficient frontload damage and block. We have early Shiv synergy and discard synergies. Look for Accuracy, Footwork, or more premium discard outlets/draw.
### F7
- [event] Foundation phase: prioritize survival. Our HP is critically low, so we must rest when possible, remove curses, and find strong early game attacks or mitigation.
- [card_select] Foundation plan: survive with efficient frontload damage and block while drawing cards. Still looking for a core scaling engine (Poison, Discard, or Shiv). Avoid adding narrow synergy pieces until an engine is found.
- [map] Foundation phase: focusing on drafting efficient damage and block to stabilize health; prioritizing survival through rest sites over risky unknown nodes given current low HP.
### F8
- [rest_site] Foundation deck with Shiv and discard synergies (Ricochet, Blade of Ink, Cloak and Dagger). Focus on playing Shivs for damage and leveraging discard (Survivor, Dagger Throw) for Ricochet. Needs more consistency in damage and block before Act 1 boss. Avoid adding more weak attacks.
### F9
- [card_reward] Foundation plan: Use frontload, Shivs, and Vulnerable (Expose) to burst down enemies. Utilize discard outlets (Dagger Throw, Survivor) to trigger Ricochet. Look for an engine piece (scaling damage/poison/shiv payoffs) while avoiding bloat.
### F10
- [map] Foundation phase: focusing on drafting efficient damage and block while prioritizing survival through rest sites over risky elites until the deck stabilizes.
### F11
- [rest_site] Foundation deck with Shiv and discard synergies (Ricochet, Blade of Ink). Prioritize consistent damage and mitigation; look for more draw/discard tools to trigger Sly effects reliably.
### F12
- [event] Foundation phase: relying on Neutralize++, Precise Cut, and Ricochet for damage while upgrading key skills and attacks. Keep adding card draw and defensive pieces for consistency.
### F13
- [card_reward] Foundation plan: leaning towards Shivs and Discard. We have Leading Strike, Blade of Ink, Cloak and Dagger, and Ricochet. Continue looking for draw (Acrobatics, Calculated Gamble), energy (Tactician), and a core scaling piece like Accuracy or strong discard payoffs. Keep the deck lean; skip off-plan commons.
### F14
- [card_reward] Foundation plan: Use frontload damage and Shivs to race enemies, while relying on efficient block and Weak (Neutralize, Leg Sweep) to survive. Looking for a core engine, likely Shiv scaling or more Sly synergy.
### F15
- [card_reward] Committed Shiv plan: scale damage with Accuracy and burst the enemy down with Shiv generators. Needs draw to find pieces quickly and block for survival while scaling. Avoid non-Shiv generic attacks to keep the deck lean.
### F16
- [rest_site] Committed Shiv plan: utilize Accuracy, Blade of Ink, and Cloak and Dagger for scaling damage, while mitigating incoming attacks with Leg Sweep and Backflip. Needs more card draw to assemble the engine faster, and consistent block.
### F17
- [card_reward] Committed Shiv/Discard plan: use Accuracy to scale Shivs and discard outlets to trigger Sly cards. Need more draw/cycle and card removal to ensure we find Accuracy and shiv generators quickly.
### F18
- [event] Foundation Shiv/Attack deck: scaling with Accuracy and using Storm of Steel/Cloak and Dagger, but needs more reliable card draw, defense, and Shiv generators. Avoid adding more basic attacks, focus on card removals for Strikes/Defends and picking up block scaling.
- [card_select] Committed Shiv/Discard plan: use discard outlets to cycle quickly and trigger Sly (Ricochet), while stacking Accuracy for massive Shiv damage. Needs more discard outlets (Acrobatics) and draw; avoid adding generic attacks that don't generate Shivs or cycle.
- [map] Foundation deck: currently building around Shiv and discard synergies; prioritize high-value block cards and card draw to cycle through basic strikes/defends, and aim to reach 500 gold for a major shop power spike.
### F19
- [card_reward] Committed Shiv plan: utilize Accuracy++ and Shiv generators (Cloak and Dagger, Blade of Ink, Storm of Steel) to output massive scaling damage while defending. Needs more draw (Acrobatics) and energy to cycle the engine.
### F20
- [card_reward] Committed Shiv plan: prioritize Shiv generators, draw to cycle them, and block/mitigation for survival. Avoid non-Shiv attacks. Look for Afterimage, Finisher, or more draw.
### F21
- [map] Foundation deck: currently high HP and gold, focusing on adding high-impact damage or scaling poison to handle Act 2's scaling requirements. Prioritize card rewards from hallway fights and prepare for a major shop spend to refine the deck.
### F22
- [card_reward] Committed shiv plan: play shiv generators to deal damage and fill the exhaust pile, then use Knife Trap as a massive finisher. Needs card draw (Acrobatics/Backflip) to cycle faster. Avoid generic attacks.
### F23
- [card_reward] Committed Shiv plan: scale damage with Accuracy and flood with Up My Sleeve/Cloak and Dagger, recurring through Knife Trap. Needs consistent card draw (Acrobatics, more Backflips) to cycle faster and Afterimage for automatic block.
### F24
- [card_select] Committed shiv plan: use Cloak and Dagger, Knife Trap, and Storm of Steel to generate shivs with Accuracy in play; prioritize draw (Backflip, Acrobatics) and energy to sustain the engine.
- [card_reward] Committed Shiv plan: prioritize Shiv generators (Blade Dance, etc.), draw to cycle them, and block/mitigation for survival. With Footwork, we have a defense engine; now look for draw (Acrobatics, Adrenaline) and energy (Tactician). Avoid poison or unrelated cards.
- [map] Foundation deck: currently high gold and healthy; focusing on card upgrades at rest sites to scale damage and preparing for a major shop visit to convert gold into late-game power.
### F25
- [rest_site] Committed Shiv engine: play Accuracy and Footwork, generate Shivs to deal massive damage, and use Knife Trap as a finisher. Defend with high Dexterity block cards. Need more card draw and to thin out basic Strikes.
### F26
- [map] Foundation deck with high gold: prioritize HP preservation and reaching the upcoming shop to transform gold into a scaling engine. Avoid Act 2 elites until a definitive win condition is established. Focus on picking up high-value block or draw cards to stabilize hallway fights.
### F27
- [card_reward] Committed Shiv plan: play Accuracy, generate Shivs, and finish with Knife Trap. Needs much more card draw (Acrobatics, Expertise) to cycle efficiently and find key pieces. Skip generic damage.
### F28
- [rest_site] Committed Shiv engine: play Accuracy and Footwork, generate Shivs to deal massive damage, and use Knife Trap as a finisher. Defend with high Dexterity block cards. Need to thin out basic Strikes and pick up card removals.
- [map] Foundation deck with high gold reserves; prioritize reaching the floor 13 shop to convert gold into scaling and card removals while avoiding unnecessary Act 2 hallway damage.
### F29
- [event] Committed shiv/weak plan: rely on Leg Sweep and Neutralize to maintain Weak with Paper Krane, block with Footwork-boosted defensive cards, and deal damage through Accuracy-boosted shivs.
- [map] Foundation deck with high gold: prioritizing HP preservation and reaching the upcoming shop to transform gold into a scaling engine while avoiding Act 2 elites.
### F30
- [card_select] Committed Shiv plan: scale damage rapidly with Accuracy and generate Shivs, using Footwork and Leg Sweep for defense. Avoid generic attacks and focus on draw and more Shiv generation.
- [map] Foundation deck: currently building around basic Silent cards and accumulated gold. Use this shop to find a definitive scaling engine like poison or shivs and remove basic Strikes to improve draw consistency for the upcoming boss.
### F31
- [card_select] Committed shiv plan: rely on Accuracy to scale Shiv damage, generating them with Cloak and Dagger, Leading Strike, and Storm of Steel. Play Defensive skills with Footwork scaling while attacking. Looking for more draw and maybe a finisher.
### F32
- [rest_site] Committed Shiv deck: play Accuracy and Phantom Blades early, generate Shivs constantly, and use Knife Trap as a massive finisher. Needs reliable draw; avoid cluttering the deck with non-synergistic attacks.

## Existing Combat Guides (relevant enemies)
[Guide: Hunter Killer] WR=95%, 42 episodes, confidence=0.90, v9
  - **Execute Early Burst:** The most effective strategy is a 1-3 round kill. Use Adrenaline and Storm of Steel to generate massive Shiv counts early, or Assassinate to bypass defensive scaling. Delaying the fight past Round 4 increases average damage taken from ~0 to over 20 HP.
- **Shred or Bypass Plating:** The enemy starts with Plating (e.g., Plating 4), reducing damage per hit. Either commit to high-frequency attacks (Shivs) to overwhelm this layer or use high-value single hits like Assassinate or Predator to ignore the flat reduction.
- **Neutralize the Multi-Hit:** If a quick kill is impossible, you must apply Weakness (Neutralize+) before the 7x3 and 5x3 attack turns. Weakness is statistically superior to standard Block cards here, as it mitigates damage across every instance of the multi-hit.
- **Manage the Vulnerable Window:** The enemy applies Vulnerable to setup its lethal 7x3 attacks. If you cannot kill the enemy before this intent, prioritize Blur or Afterimage to stack block, as face-tanking while Vulnerable leads to immediate defeat (avg. loss round 2.5).
[Guide: Infested Prism] WR=70%, 20 episodes, confidence=0.88, v21
  - **The Turn 5 Threshold:** Prism acts as a strict damage check, spiking in lethality at Turn 5 (+4 Strength) and Turn 9 (+8 Strength). You must either burst the boss down by Turn 4 (via Accuracy/Shiv combos) or set up heavy, continuous mitigation.
- **Weak is Mandatory:** The enemy's massive multi-hit 'Whirlwind' patterns will instantly overwhelm standard block once buffed. Ensure you carry and hold sources of Weak (Neutralize+, Leg Sweep, Sucker Punch) specifically to blunt the Turn 5+ onslaught.
- **Scaling Defense:** In fights extending beyond the Turn 4 window, passive block is essential. Prioritize playing Footwork, Blur, and Afterimage in your early cycles so your block naturally paces the multi-attacks.
- **Early HP Management:** Because of high initial attack values (frequently swinging for 22+ early on), entering this fight at low HP is extremely dangerous. Play defensively from Turn 1, applying Weak before over-committing to early damage.
[Guide: Lagavulin Matriarch] WR=56%, 32 episodes, confidence=0.90, v32
  - **Capitalize on the Sleep Phase:** Use turns 1-3 strictly for non-damaging setup. Play Powers (`Footwork`, `Afterimage`), filter your deck, and use `Blur` to stack block. Do not attack into her Plating early.
- **Treat Awake Phase as a DPS Race:** Once she wakes and Plating drops, the fight becomes a strict damage race. Unleash your Shivs, Poison, and high-value attacks immediately. Fights extending past round 13 consistently end in defeat.
- **Counter Multi-Hits with Precision:** Her multi-hit attacks (8x2, 11x2) become rapidly lethal as she gains Strength. Hold Weakness (`Neutralize+`, `Leg Sweep`) and Strength-down effects (`Piercing Wail`) strictly for these multi-hit turns to survive the damage race.
[Guide: Living Fog] WR=88%, 34 episodes, confidence=0.90, v25
  - **Push for the 4-Round Threshold:** The cleanest wins (0-3 HP loss) conclude by Round 4. Aggressively use Rounds 1 and 2 to deal damage or play high-impact setup cards like Assassinate or Predator, as the Fog is entirely passive during this window.
- **The Turn 4 Pivot:** If the Fog survives to R4, expect a 16-damage burst (8 from Fog, 8 from Bomb DeathBlow). This is the highest threat window. Prioritize Weakening the Fog with Neutralize and using high-value blocks like Survivor or Flick-Flack here.
- **Odd-Turn Breathers:** Use R3 and R5 (Summoning turns) to cycle your deck or play powers. Since the Fog does not attack while summoning, you can safely ignore the newly spawned Gas Bomb and focus on either Fog damage or hand setup.
- **Ignore the Minions:** Data shows that targeting Gas Bombs is usually a trap unless using incidental AoE. The fastest wins ignore the bombs entirely, accepting the R4/R6 DeathBlow damage while racing to deplete the Fog's HP.
- **Weakness Management:** Be prepared for the Weak debuff applied by expiring Gas Bombs. Try to frontload your highest damage attacks in R1-R3 before the R4 Weakness triggers.
[Guide: Skulking Colony] WR=70%, 23 episodes, confidence=0.90, v19
  - **Survive the Opening Volley:** The Colony threatens massive burst damage immediately (up to 19 or 9x2). Losses happen remarkably fast (average 2.1 rounds) when players prioritize basic attacks over blocking. Play Defend, Survivor, and Footwork early to stabilize.
- **Bypass the Hardened Shell:** The enemy regenerates 15 Block every round. Do not waste energy on basic attacks trying to break this armor. Rely on Poison (Deadly Poison, Poisoned Stab) to bypass the Block entirely and deal passive HP damage while you dedicate energy to defense.
- **Neutralize Strength Spikes:** The Colony gains Strength at Round 4 and Round 8, turning its multi-attacks into lethal threats. Hold or time your Weak applications (Neutralize) to ensure the enemy is debuffed during these enraged turns.
- **Capitalize on Passive Turns:** The enemy occasionally uses safe Defend or Buff intents. Reserve these windows to deploy scaling powers (Footwork, Afterimage) or setup cards (Well-Laid Plans) without bleeding health.
[Guide: Sludge Spinner] WR=100%, 68 episodes, confidence=0.90, v41

[Deck Guide: shiv] memories=80, confidence=0.90, v23
  - **Prioritize Draw & Energy:** Shiv decks rely on playing many low-cost cards, which quickly empties the hand. Without robust card cycle (Expertise, Calculated Gamble) and energy generation (Adrenaline, Tactician), the engine will stall. Expertise is particularly strong as Shiv generators and 0-cost cards (Neutralize, Hidden Daggers) allow you to consistently empty your hand for maximum draw.
- **Scale Block Proactively:** Basic Defends will fail you, even with Footwork. Layer premium mitigation like Leg Sweep, Piercing Wail, and Dash to survive mid-to-late game damage checks.
- **Focus Your Damage:** Commit to Accuracy early to ensure cards like Blade Dance, Fan of Knives, and Cloak and Dagger deal scaling burst damage.
- **Solve Early AoE:** Multi-enemy encounters punish single-target shiv builds heavily. Secure upfront AoE early in Act 1 to avoid being overwhelmed before your shiv engine is fully online.

## Card Notes (seen this run)
- Neutralize: A-tier starter; upgrade is premium. Save for big attack turns and boss burst checks. 0-cost Weak often beats a Strike; don’t fire it on non-attack intents unless it changes lethal.
- Survivor: C-tier starter block. Fine early and with discard synergies, but with Well-Laid Plans do not auto-retain it over rarer swing cards, scaling, or premium defense.
- Ricochet: Sly: plays for free when discarded by a card effect. 2-cost: 4 hits × 3 damage = 12 base (upgraded: 4 × 4 = 16). Does NOT benefit from Accuracy — Accuracy only boosts Shivs, and Ricochet is not a Shiv. Effective cost is 0 energy via discard outlets. Each hit benefits from Strength.
- Cloak and Dagger: 1-cost Skill: 6 Block, generates 1 Shiv (Upgraded: 2). High-tier foundational piece for Shiv engines, scaling defensively with Dexterity (Footwork) and offensively with Accuracy. The upgrade is extremely high priority as it doubles the Shiv output. Keep in mind it plays 2-3 cards total, making it susceptible to Beat of Death and Time Eater restrictions later in runs.
- Leading Strike: 1-cost Attack: Deals damage and adds 1 Shiv to your hand. Provides solid immediate frontloaded damage while acting as a generator for Shiv synergies (Accuracy, Fan of Knives, Finisher). It offers immediate impact compared to purely generator cards like Cloak and Dagger, making it strong in early Act 1 where raw damage is necessary to burst down Elites.
- Dagger Throw: 1-cost: 9 damage + draw 1 + discard 1. The discard is a card effect, triggering Sly cards (Reflex, Tactician, Untouchable) for free plays. Cycles deck while dealing damage. Flat 9 damage — does not scale with build progression.
- Precise Cut: 0-cost: deals 13 damage minus 2 per other card in hand. Strongest in small hands (1-2 other cards = 9-11 damage for 0 energy). Empty hand = 13 free damage. Pair with hand-emptying effects (Restlessness, Calculated Gamble).
- Backflip: 1-cost: block + draw 2. Defends and cycles simultaneously. The draw does not trigger Sly (draw is not discard). Pairs with Dexterity (Footwork) for scaled Block.
- Expose: Expose removes ALL Block and Artifact. Do NOT waste it on Turn 1 against bosses that generate massive shields on Turn 2 (e.g., Ceremonial Beast's 150-Block Plow). Hold it until the enemy actually has the block or artifact you need to strip. Do not burn it blindly just for 2 Vulnerable.
- Leg Sweep: 2-cost: high Block + applies Weak. Scales with Dexterity for the Block portion. Pounce reduces the next Skill cost to 0 — play Pounce before Leg Sweep to play it for free.
- Accuracy: Power: +4 damage to all Shivs per copy. Base Shiv = 4 dmg → 8 with 1 copy, 12 with 2 copies. ONLY buffs Shiv cards — does NOT affect Ricochet, Dagger Spray, or other multi-hit attacks. Stacks: multiple copies multiply value linearly with Shiv generators (Blade Dance, Up My Sleeve, Infinite Blades, Fan of Knives).
- Storm of Steel: Discards your ENTIRE hand to generate Shivs. This destroys Retained cards (Well-Laid Plans), Nightmared copies, and defensive tools held for future turns. NEVER play this if you are holding essential mitigation (Apparition, Piercing Wail). Best used to convert unplayable cards, statuses (Slimed), or basic strikes into damage. Excellent synergy with Tingsha or Tough Bandages.
- Knife Trap: Replays EVERY Shiv in Exhaust Pile for free. Functions as a burst finisher, but CAUTION: Base Shivs only deal 4 damage. Replaying 5 Shivs is merely 20 damage. To be a boss-killing win condition, Knife Trap strictly requires Accuracy to boost Shiv base damage OR massive Shiv density. In thick decks (>25 cards) with only a few generators, it is too slow and low-impact.
- Up My Sleeve: 2-cost Skill: Generates 3 Shivs (Upgraded: 4). Cost reduces by 1 each time it is played in combat. At 0-cost, it becomes the most efficient Shiv generator available. Premium engine for longer fights (bosses/elites). Pairs exceptionally well with Accuracy, Fan of Knives, and Afterimage. Because it initially costs 2, prioritize playing it during safe enemy setup windows.
- Footwork: Power: permanent +2 Dexterity (upgraded: +3). All Block cards gain +2/+3 Block for rest of combat. Stacks with multiple copies. Unlike Anticipate, this is permanent. Upgrade from +2 to +3 is a significant boost.
- Acrobatics: A-: premium filtering; much better with Runic Pyramid, discard synergies, or retained junk. On dangerous turns play it before filler attacks to dig for block or Wail. Take often.
- Phantom Blades: Power: Your first Shiv played each turn deals bonus damage (+6). ALL Shivs Retain. This is primarily a combo/burst enabler, not just passive scaling. By hoarding 0-cost Shivs in hand over multiple turns, you can unleash massive zero-energy burst to push specific boss phases, bypass alternating immunities (like Test Subject's Nemesis), or secure lethal. High priority in Shiv decks.
- Dodge and Roll: 1-cost: gain Block this turn and next turn. Provides Block over 2 turns, unlike Defend which is 1 turn only. Scales with Dexterity for both applications.
